$comment: Module taxonomy for the AI slide-authoring prompt. TWO LEVELS: groups (top) each own a set of tags (leaf). A module's <tags> (in its .mdpmod.xml) place it: its FIRST tag decides its group; the rest are secondary/cross-listing. A workspace can override this per folder with .mdp/taxonomy.json (same shape; if it has a 'groups' array it fully replaces this default). Array order = display order. Edit freely — this is data, not code.

groups.label: Math & science
groups.desc: equations, proofs, geometry, function/coordinate plots, logic, sets & probability, matrices, sequences

groups.label: Charts & data
groups.desc: quantitative charts and stat displays built from your numbers (bars, distributions, KPIs, comparisons)

groups.label: Diagrams & flows
groups.desc: relationships, processes, hierarchies, cycles, timelines and node graphs

groups.label: Layout & structure
groups.desc: arranging content on the slide: multi-column, grids, cards, free-placed boxes, grouping

groups.label: Content & callouts
groups.desc: emphasized text blocks and media: callouts, notes, quotes, lists, references, figures/images, tables

groups.label: UI & inline elements
groups.desc: small chrome: badges, buttons, device/app mockups, inline marks, decorations, interactive widgets

groups.label: Styling & utilities
groups.desc: apply CSS classes or inline styles to a slide or region (no visible element of their own)

groups.label: Other
groups.desc: modules whose tags are not in any group above
